Patient Handout

Medication Instructions

GLP-1 Medication Guide

Your GLP-1 medication is injected once weekly. Follow these instructions carefully to get the most benefit and minimize side effects.

Starting dose schedule — Dr. Leff will guide increases from there:

0.25 mg weekly
Weeks 1–4
or
0.5 mg weekly
Week 5+

How to Inject

Approved Injection Sites

Rotate sites each week — never inject in the same spot twice in a row.

  1. 1
    Use the pre-filled auto-injector pen provided by your pharmacy.
  2. 2
    Inject into your abdomen, thigh, or upper arm — rotate sites each week.
  3. 3
    Pinch the skin, insert the needle at 90 degrees, press the button, and hold for 10 seconds.
  4. 4
    Do not inject into muscle — stay in fatty tissue just under the skin.
  5. 5
    Change the needle after every use.

Common side effects (usually improve after 2–4 weeks):

Nausea — eat smaller, blander meals; avoid fatty or spicy food. Fatigue — stay hydrated. Constipation — increase fiber and water intake.

Call us if you experience: severe abdominal pain that does not go away, signs of low blood sugar (if diabetic), or a rash or allergic reaction at the injection site.

Storage

Keep in the refrigerator at 36–46°F. May be left at room temperature for up to 28 days. Do not freeze. Protect from light.
